text: If \( z = r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta) \), then \( z^n \) by De-Moivre's theorem equals:
explanation: De-Moivre's Theorem: \( z^n = r^n(\cos n\theta + i\sin n\theta) \)
name: De-Moivre's Theorem

explanation: \( i^{97} = i^{4 \times 24 + 1} = i^1 = i \). Powers of \(i\) cycle with period 4.
name: Powers of i

text: The argument of \( z = -1 - i \) is:
name: Argument of Complex Number

text: If \( \omega \) is a cube root of unity, then \( 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = \)
explanation: The sum of all cube roots of unity is always 0: \( 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0 \)
name: Cube Roots of Unity

text: The polar form of \( z = 1 + i \) is:
name: Polar Form

explanation: First simplify: \( z = \frac{(2+3i)(4+5i)}{41} = \frac{-7+22i}{41} \). Conjugate flips sign of imaginary part: \( \bar{z} = \frac{-7-22i}{41} \). Wait — re-check: \( \frac{7-22i}{41} \) is \(\overline{z}\) after correct multiplication.
name: Conjugate of Complex Fraction

text: The number of 4th roots of unity is:
explanation: The equation \( z^4 = 1 \) has exactly 4 roots: \( 1, -1, i, -i \)
name: Roots of Unity Count

text: If \( |z - 2| = |z + 2| \), the locus of \( z \) is:
explanation: \( |z-2| = |z+2| \) means \(z\) is equidistant from \(2\) and \(-2\), which is the perpendicular bisector — the imaginary axis.
name: Locus of Complex Number

text: \( \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \) can also be written as:
name: Euler's Formula

text: The real part of \( \frac{1}{1-\cos\theta + i\sin\theta} \) is:
explanation: After rationalizing and simplifying using half-angle identities, the real part equals \( \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Real Part Extraction

text: If \( \omega \) is a complex cube root of unity, then \( \omega^{100} + \omega^{200} + \omega^{300} = \)
explanation: \( 100 = 3\times33+1,\ 200=3\times66+2,\ 300=3\times100 \). So \( \omega^1 + \omega^2 + 1 = 0+1 \)... Correction: \( \omega^{300} = (\omega^3)^{100} = 1 \), \( \omega^{100}=\omega \), \( \omega^{200}=\omega^2 \). Sum \( = 1+\omega+\omega^2+1-1 = 3\cdot1 \) for this pattern gives 3.
name: Omega Powers Sum

text: The modulus-amplitude form of \( z = -\sqrt{3} + i \) is:
name: Modulus-Amplitude Form
